pr.title: Link and ninja apply HSD Bytecode
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Marked sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c as Matching in configure.py and updated GALE01 symbol metadata so HSD ByteCode-related constants/strings are treated as local compiler-style labels rather than global HSD_ByteCode_* or lbl_* symbols. The PR appears to make the bytecode translation unit linkable/matching with ninja by aligning symbol visibility and generated label names with the expected object layout.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).

In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, replaced several global data labels near 0x804074F0-0x80407574 named lbl_804074F0 through lbl_80407574 with local numbered string/object labels @393, @394, @395, @398, @399, @404, and @407.
Added a local label entry ...data.0 at .data:0x804074F0 alongside @393.
Changed HSD_ByteCode_804D5F98 and HSD_ByteCode_804D5F9C in .sdata to local labels @396 and @397, with @396 size adjusted from 0x4 to 0x1 and @397 retaining the 0x6 string data classification.

Changed HSD_ByteCode_804DE7A0 through HSD_ByteCode_804DE7D0 in .sdata2 from global HSD_ByteCode_* names to local numbered constants @392, @400, @401, @402, @403, @405, @406, and @409 while preserving float/double alignment and sizes.
smart_moves: Kept the configure.py matching switch minimal: only bytecode.c moved from NonMatching to Matching, reducing risk to neighboring baselib objects.

Converted anonymous constants and strings to local @NNN labels instead of keeping artificial global HSD_ByteCode_* names, which better reflects compiler-emitted local constants for a matched translation unit.

Preserved section addresses, sizes, alignments, and data classifications for most constants while only changing symbol names/scope, indicating the fix targeted linker/symbol ownership rather than data layout.
Adjusted the .sdata symbol at 0x804D5F98 to a 1-byte local object, suggesting attention to exact object sizing rather than relying on a coarse placeholder global.
decomp_lessons: When promoting a source file to Matching, audit symbols.txt for that translation unit's local constants and strings; stale global placeholder names can prevent clean linking or obscure ownership.
HSD baselib files may require local @NNN labels for compiler-generated strings, floats, and doubles rather than manually named HSD_* globals.
A matching status change in configure.py may depend entirely on symbol-map cleanup even when no C source diff is needed.
For sdata2 float/double constants, retain exact align:4 or align:8 annotations and sizes while renaming or localizing symbols.
Do not assume address-derived lbl_* names should remain global once a unit is matched; they may need to become local string labels.
naming_conventions: Local compiler-generated constants and strings are represented as @NNN labels, such as @392, @393, @400, and @409.
Artificial address labels like lbl_804074F0 were replaced with numbered local labels when the data belonged to bytecode.c.
HSD_ByteCode_804D5F98, HSD_ByteCode_804D5F9C, and HSD_ByteCode_804DE7A0-style global names were removed in favor of local @NNN symbols.
Local section labels use the ...data.0 convention, as shown at .data:0x804074F0 and already present near hsdClass.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote an object from NonMatching to Matching only after symbol visibility and local-label ownership line up with the expected linked object.
Use symbols.txt scope changes from global to local to make anonymous constants/strings resolve as part of the matched translation unit.
For small-data sections, verify exact object sizes; the .sdata object at 0x804D5F98 changed from size 0x4 to size 0x1.
Preserve literal pool order and addresses in .sdata2 while renaming symbols to local @NNN labels.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No human PR body text was included in the provided context, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from the changed files.
searchable_terms: Link and ninja apply HSD Bytecode
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c for any source-level cleanup after it became Matching.
Search for other HSD_* placeholder globals in symbols.txt that should become local @NNN labels when their translation units are matched.
Check build history around ninja apply/link failures for baselib bytecode to understand whether symbol scope was the only blocker.
